children.name: prevent an element from exceeding the screen and ever overflowing
use `pointer-events: none` to disable mouse events of an element. can be useful when two elements are stacked on top of each other, and you want to allow the events of the element beneath through

code: pointer-events: none; /* click + hover are disabled for this */

children.name: catch multiple class of exceptions in one block

text: - error handling in decorators can be difficult. is there is a try block inside the decorator, and the calling function was passed with incorrect parameters, the traceback only show the try block inside the decorator. the calling function, which called the decorated function with wrong parameters, will not be shown.

children.name: the takeaway might be to always raise exceptions in a decorator

code: 4	from typing import Callable, TypeVar, ParamSpec, Any
9	    decorator(a=2) # decorator will call `foo`` with wrong parameters, but the main invoker is this function
15	        # the traceback will be printed here, but it will not show that `calling_function` passed the values to `decorator`
